text.title: Coverage Rebuild Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Coverage Rebuild Playbook. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Files are ranked by risk × churn; top band is tested first.
rule.rationale: Files are ranked by risk × churn; top band is tested first.

rule.statement: Initial tests are characterization (pin current behaviour) — not aspirational TDD.
rule.rationale: Initial tests are characterization (pin current behaviour) — not aspirational TDD.

rule.statement: Coverage delta is measured weekly; missed milestones trigger scope reduction.
rule.rationale: Coverage delta is measured weekly; missed milestones trigger scope reduction.

rule.statement: CI fails any commit that lowers coverage below the rolling floor.
rule.rationale: CI fails any commit that lowers coverage below the rolling floor.

rule.statement: Every test names the production path it covers; tests that exist only to lift % are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Every test names the production path it covers; tests that exist only to lift % are forbidden.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-coverage-rebuild-playbook.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
